paper_title: Pollen Collection and Analysis from O. lignaria Nests

methods.description: Collection of pollen from O. lignaria nests at three different sites to study the diversity and protein content of pollen provisions.

steps.description: We collected pollen from O. lignaria nests at three sites in late March 2017.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from O. lignaria nests at specified sites

steps.description: Following the completion of nesting activity in July 2017, straws were collected, and pollen samples were removed and stored at -80°C until processed (~8 wk).
steps.searchable_summary: store collected pollen samples at -80°C for later processing

methods.description: Homogenization of pollen samples and pilot study to test the effect of drying on protein quantification.

steps.description: Pollen samples at each site were homogenized using a mortar and pestle.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize pollen samples using mortar and pestle

steps.description: We first conducted a pilot study to test the effect of drying pollen prior to protein quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pilot study to assess drying effect on protein quantification

steps.description: Half of the samples were then randomly chosen to be dried at 36°C for 24 hr, while the remaining samples were left undried.
steps.searchable_summary: dry half of the pollen samples at 36°C for 24 hours

methods.description: Experimental trials to quantify protein content in pollen samples using different buffers and weights.

steps.description: We then extracted 5, 10, and 20 mg samples from the homogenized pollen samples at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: extract specific weights of pollen samples for analysis

steps.description: Samples were randomly assigned one of the two buffers or DW (0.01-M PBS, 0.1-M NaOH).
steps.searchable_summary: assign buffers to pollen samples for protein quantification

steps.description: All experiments involved the homogenization of samples prior to analysis, including vortexing and mixture using a micropestle, for the purpose of resuspending settled pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: vortex and mix pollen samples to resuspend settled pollen

steps.description: Each experimental trial was repeated three times.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat experimental trials three times for validation
